_doc: Grounded IMU/wheel sensor parameters for the IPEx rover, extrapolated from real open planetary-rover data. PACKAGED (solnav.config/data) so the installed wheel can locate it. No fabricated values; provenance per field. See validation/g1_imu_wheel_data_sources.md.

platform.name: IPEx (ISRU Pilot Excavator) class
platform.provenance: NTRS 20240008162 (Schuler et al., IPEx TRL-5) via ipex_specs

imu.provenance: MADMAX rover IMU (Meyer et al. 2021); MTi 10-series output specs (mtidocs.xsens.com/output-specifications). MEMS noise is sensor-class-bound -> transfers to IPEx directly.
imu.white_noise_provenance: [ASSUMPTION] per-sample white-noise sigma = noise_density * sqrt(rate) treats the sample rate as the effective noise bandwidth; the real anti-alias bandwidth differs, so this is a modeling assumption, not a datasheet value.

accel.range_provenance: MTi-10 high-range full-range option (~20 g = 200 m/s^2; mtidocs.xsens.com/output-specifications); corrected from the 50 m/s^2 default.

bias_model.correlation_time_provenance: [ASSUMPTION] not in the MTi-10 spec; 1e3 s is a conventional MEMS in-run value.
bias_model.stationary_sigma_provenance: [ASSUMPTION] the in-run bias stability (Allan-deviation floor) is mapped to the Gauss-Markov stationary sigma; this equivalence is a modeling assumption, not a spec value. Replaces a pure random walk (which over-drifts).
bias_model.note: white-noise sigma per sample = noise_density * sqrt(rate) (see white_noise_provenance); bias is GM with stationary sigma = in_run_bias_stability (see stationary_sigma_provenance)

wheel_odometry.encoder_counts_per_rev_provenance: [ASSUMPTION] typical 12-bit wheel-encoder resolution; no IPEx encoder spec is published.
wheel_odometry.yaw_source: differential wheel speeds (skid-steer), derived from noisy per-wheel encoders (NOT true yaw rate)
wheel_odometry.error_model: each wheel encoder reads wheel speed = ground/(1-slip); forward speed and yaw rate over-read by slip. slip is the dustgym terramechanics solve (NOT exposed on the sensor sample).

acceptance_envelope.label: MER autonomous-navigation design goal / contextual check (NOT a universal measured soil-error law)
acceptance_envelope.provenance: MER autonomous-navigation design goal / contextual check (Maimone, Cheng, Matthies 2007, MER visual odometry). The ~10% figure is a design goal and a contextual sanity check for model faithfulness, NOT a universal measured soil-error law.
